Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply a recommended bactericide or copper-based treatment.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation, improve field sanitation, and disinfect tools regularly.

Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Remove affected foliage and apply an appropriate fungicide.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops, mulch soil, and reduce leaf wetness.

Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Remove infected tissue immediately and apply a suitable fungicide.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Improve airflow, avoid prolonged leaf wetness, and monitor humidity conditions.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Prune infected leaves and use an approved fungicide.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Reduce greenhouse humidity, space plants properly, and improve ventilation.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply a protective fungicide.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Avoid splashing soil onto leaves, rotate crops, and maintain field hygiene.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Apply insecticidal soap or an appropriate miticide.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Monitor plants regularly, reduce plant stress, and control dusty dry conditions.

Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply a suitable fungicide.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Improve spacing, reduce humidity, and avoid prolonged leaf moisture.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: Remove severely infected plants and control whitefly vectors.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use resistant varieties, insect barriers, and strict vector management.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: Remove infected plants; there is no direct chemical cure.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Disinfect tools, avoid contaminated handling, and remove infected plant material early.

Tomato___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper watering, nutrition, hygiene, and regular monitoring.
